Operation 'deleteObject' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_side_effects, not_actionable. Original summary and description are extremely vague and lack actionable detail. Enhanced version clarifies: the specific action (delete from container), authentication requirement, the permanent nature of deletion, versioning behavior, and expected HTTP responses. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description: Delete object
data.summary_suggestion: Delete an object from a container
data.description_suggestion: Deletes a specific object from the specified container within the account. Requires authentication via Keystone token. The object is permanently removed and cannot be recovered unless versioning is enabled on the container. Returns HTTP 204 No Content on successful deletion. Attempting to delete a non-existent object returns HTTP 404 Not Found.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are extremely vague and lack actionable detail. Enhanced version clarifies: the specific action (delete from container), authentication requirement, the permanent nature of deletion, versioning behavior, and expected HTTP responses. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

message: Operation 'bulkDelete' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is adequate, but description lacks critical details about input format (newline-delimited paths), response structure (BulkDeleteResponse with counts and errors), and operational context (versioning behavior, middleware requirement). Enhanced description provides AI agents with clear understanding of how to format requests, interpret responses, and understand the operation's constraints and effects.

data.current_description: Delete multiple objects in a single request using the bulk middleware.
data.description_suggestion: Deletes multiple objects in a single request using the bulk delete middleware. Accepts a newline-delimited list of object paths in the format 'container/object' in the request body. Returns a BulkDeleteResponse containing counts of successfully deleted objects, failures, and detailed error information for any failed deletions. Objects are permanently removed unless versioning is enabled. Requires authentication and the bulk middleware must be enabled on the Swift cluster.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate, but description lacks critical details about input format (newline-delimited paths), response structure (BulkDeleteResponse with counts and errors), and operational context (versioning behavior, middleware requirement). Enhanced description provides AI agents with clear understanding of how to format requests, interpret responses, and understand the operation's constraints and effects.

data.current_summary: Show account details and list containers
data.current_description: Shows details for an account and lists containers, sorted by name.
data.description_suggestion: Shows details for an account and lists containers sorted by name. Returns account metadata (container count, object count, bytes used) in response headers and a list of container objects in the response body. Supports filtering and pagination via query parameters.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity about what specific account details are returned and how they are returned (headers vs body). Added information about response structure and available filtering/pagination options to improve AI agent understanding of the operation's outputs.

message: Operation 'updateAccountMetadata' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. data.current_summary: Create, update, or delete account metadata
data.current_description: Creates, updates, or deletes account metadata using X-Account-Meta-{name} headers.
data.description_suggestion: Creates, updates, or deletes account metadata using X-Account-Meta-{name} headers. Metadata is set via request headers; to delete metadata, send an empty value for the header. Changes take effect immediately and are reflected in subsequent HEAD requests. Requires authentication token.
data.reasoning: Description mentions how to set metadata but doesn't explain how to delete it or clarify the side effects. Added information about deletion mechanism, timing of changes, and authentication requirements for better AI agent guidance on operation usage and effects.

message: Operation 'deleteAccount' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_side_effects. Description mentions authentication requirements but lacks critical context about the destructive nature of the operation and preconditions. Added information about cascading deletion of containers/objects, irreversibility, and the requirement that the account be empty to help AI agents understand the severity and prerequisites of this operation.

data.current_description: Deletes the specified account. Requires reseller admin token and allow_account_management config.
data.description_suggestion: Deletes the specified account and all associated containers and objects. Requires reseller admin token and allow_account_management configuration to be enabled. This is a destructive operation with no recovery option. Account must be empty or deletion will fail.
data.reasoning: Description mentions authentication requirements but lacks critical context about the destructive nature of the operation and preconditions. Added information about cascading deletion of containers/objects, irreversibility, and the requirement that the account be empty to help AI agents understand the severity and prerequisites of this operation.

data.current_description: Creates a container. Does not require a request body and ignores any content.

data.current_description: Deletes an empty container.
data.description_suggestion: Deletes an empty container from the account. The container must contain no objects; attempting to delete a non-empty container will fail with a 409 Conflict error. Once deleted, the container name becomes available for reuse. Requires authentication.

Added information about the empty container requirement, error behavior for non-empty containers, and the consequence that the container name becomes available for reuse. data.current_description: Downloads the object content and gets the object metadata.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves an object's content and metadata from a container. data.current_description: Creates or replaces an object and its metadata.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new object or replaces an existing object in a container. Object content is provided in the request body; metadata is specified via X-Object-Meta-* headers. Returns the object's ETag in the response header. If an object with the same name exists, it is completely replaced. Supports large object uploads via multipart mechanism.
data.reasoning: Description lacks specifics about input format and response. Added clarity on how content and metadata are provided, what is returned (ETag), the replacement behavior, and mention of large object support. This helps AI agents understand how to construct requests and interpret responses.

data.current_description: Create or update object metadata
data.description_suggestion: Modifies an object's custom metadata without changing its content. Metadata is specified via X-Object-Meta-* headers in the request. Returns updated object metadata in response headers. Only metadata is affected; the object's content and Content-Type remain unchanged. Changes are immediately reflected.
data.reasoning: Description is repetitive and lacks essential operational details. data.current_description: Show object metadata
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves an object's metadata without downloading its content. Returns object properties in response headers including Content-Type, Content-Length, Last-Modified, ETag, and all custom X-Object-Meta-* headers. Does not return the object's data.
data.reasoning: Description is too minimal. Added clarity about what metadata is returned (system properties and custom headers), explicitly noted that content is not returned, and specified the header-based response format. Helps AI agents understand this is a lightweight metadata-only operation.

Returns a BulkDeleteResponse containing counts of successfully deleted objects, failures, and detailed error information for any failed deletions. Objects are permanently removed unless versioning is enabled. Requires authentication and the bulk middleware must be enabled on the Swift cluster.

Enhanced description provides AI agents with clear understanding of how to format requests, interpret responses, and understand the operation's constraints and effects.
